title: Exponentials and Logarithms
description: Exponential functions and the number e, logarithms as their inverse and the laws of logs, and solving exponential/logarithmic equations including growth and decay models.
objectives: Sketch and interpret graphs of $y = a^x$ for growth ($a>1$) and decay ($0<a<1$), and recognise the special number $e$
Understand logarithms as the inverse of exponentials, and convert fluently between index and log form
Apply the laws of logarithms (product, quotient, power) to simplify and combine expressions, including $\ln x$
Solve exponential equations by taking logarithms of both sides, and solve logarithmic equations using the laws of logs
Set up and use exponential growth/decay models of the form $A = A_0 e^{kt}$ to answer questions about population growth, radioactive decay and compound interest

lessons.title: Exponential functions and the number e
Logarithms and the laws of logs
Solving exponential/log equations and growth-decay models
